cases.focus: autoloaded alloptions lookup, home fallback to siteurl, and URL untrailing behavior
missing option default and notoptions cache population
pre_option, default_option, and option filters with argument capture
batch priming existing and missing options into options/notoptions caches
registered option group priming delegates into wp_prime_option_caches()
insert, serialization, alloptions versus individual cache placement, and add hooks
value mutation, explicit autoload transition, cache migration, and update hooks
bulk autoload transitions, nonexistent option false result, and cache invalidation
single and bulk wrapper return contracts
delete result, alloptions/options cache cleanup, notoptions insertion, and delete hooks
option value retrieval plus esc_attr output contract
explicit values, default autoload filter, allowed autoload values, and size heuristic

native_boundaries.reason: The probe supplies a deterministic wpdb option-table test double so option.php storage/cache decisions run without a real database. Full query preparation, SQL execution, and storage-engine behavior remain WPHX-305.
alloptions, notoptions, and individual option caches use WordPress's native WP_Object_Cache runtime and public wp_cache_* functions.
pre_option/default_option/option filters and add/update/delete option actions must remain native PHP callbacks with WordPress-compatible argument ordering.
form_option() retrieves options and delegates output escaping through esc_attr(), which reaches KSES entity normalization owned by the WPHX-303 security/escaping surface.
Option values are stored through maybe_serialize()/maybe_unserialize(), so serialized scalar and array shapes remain PHP-native until WPHX-304.05 isolates serialization parity.

remaining_gaps.detail: The candidate side is a copied WordPress oracle source tree until selected pure option/cache helpers move to Haxe parity candidates.
This fixture constrains wpdb to deterministic option-table behavior. Full wpdb query preparation, DB errors, result objects, and storage parity are a separate WPHX-305 domain.
Site/network options, site transients, and persistent object-cache drop-in behavior remain separate WPHX-304 fixture slices.
This fixture covers seed traces. Full upstream option PHPUnit parity remains a domain-level closure requirement.
